name: SIRT6 overexpression reduced frailty and extended mouse lifespan
summary: SIRT6 overexpression supplies a strong mammalian chromatin/deacylase lifespan signal.
statement: Whole-body SIRT6 overexpression in B6 mice reduced frailty and extended lifespan in both males and females while preserving age-associated glucose and energy homeostasis.
population_or_model: Male and female B6 SIRT6-transgenic mice
time_horizon: Lifetime survival and old-age physiology follow-up
quantitative_note: The authors reported median lifespan extension of 27% in males and 15% in females in this B6 background.
caveats: This is transgenic overexpression, not a clinically tested SIRT6 activator.
SIRT6 has broad DNA repair, metabolism, inflammation, and chromatin roles, so histone deacylation is not isolated as the only mechanism.
Human pathway-engagement and safety data for aging contexts are not established.
